summary: Iteration 15 Testing COMPLETED: Hero slides per-page assignment and custom page rendering verified. Fixed missing useMemo import in HomePage.js. Backend 20/20 tests passed. Frontend features working correctly.

fixed_issues.issue: Missing useMemo import causing 'useMemo is not defined' error
fixed_issues.fix: Added useMemo to React import statement

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/public/hero-slides returns all active slides
Backend: GET /api/public/hero-slides?page=home returns slides assigned to home
Backend: GET /api/public/hero-slides?page=news returns slides assigned to news
Backend: GET /api/public/hero-slides?page=gallery returns slides assigned to gallery
Backend: GET /api/public/hero-slides?page=nonexistent returns empty list
Backend: GET /api/public/site-pages returns system + custom pages
Backend: GET /api/public/nav-pages returns all nav pages
Backend: KLS page exists in nav_pages with url=/kls
Backend: Terms page has login_required=true
Backend: Privacy page has login_required=false
Backend: Hero slides assigned to KLS page ID
Backend: Admin login with admin@consultant.com / Admin123!
Backend: Wrong password correctly rejected with 401
Backend: GET /api/admin/hero-slides returns all slides
Backend: GET /api/admin/nav-pages returns all nav pages
Backend: GET /api/public/hero returns 200
Backend: GET /api/public/about returns 200
Backend: GET /api/public/services returns 200
Backend: GET /api/public/sections returns 200
Backend: GET /api/public/testimonials returns 200
Frontend: Homepage loads with hero section (3 slides)
Frontend: KLS page (/kls) renders with hero slider and page content
Frontend: KLS page shows 'summary de prueba' and 'content de prueba'
Frontend: News page (/news) shows hero slides (2 dots for 2 slides)
Frontend: Gallery page (/gallery) shows hero slide
Frontend: Terms page (/terms) shows Login Required screen when not logged in
Frontend: Terms page has 'Login to Continue' button
Frontend: Privacy page (/privacy) loads for unauthenticated users
Frontend: Unknown URL (/nonexistent) shows 404 page
Frontend: Admin login works with admin@consultant.com / Admin123!
Frontend: Admin Pages Manager loads with Custom Pages (3) and System Pages (4) tabs
Frontend: System Pages tab shows Home, News, Gallery, Reading List
Frontend: Hero Slide Form has Page Assignment section
Frontend: Page Assignment shows checkboxes for Home, News, Gallery, Reading List, Terms, Privacy, KLS
critical_code_review_comments: Console warnings about HTML hydration errors (span inside tbody/select/option) - these are from Emergent's development tooling and don't affect production
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 15 verified hero slides per-page assignment and custom page rendering. Key features: (1) HeroSection component extracted to /app/frontend/src/components/HeroSection.js as shared component. (2) DynamicPage.js finds pages by URL path and fetches hero slides assigned to that page. (3) SystemPageHero component in App.js renders hero slides on system pages (news, gallery, reading-list). (4) Catch-all route renders PageProtectedRoute+DynamicPage for custom URLs like /kls. (5) Page Assignment section in Hero Slide Form shows checkboxes for all system and custom pages. Current data: 'Welcome to Legacy' slide assigned to home, news, KLS. 'Hero 3' slide assigned to home, news, gallery. Terms page has login_required=true. KLS page has URL=/kls with content 'summary de prueba' and 'content de prueba'.

hero_per_page_assignment.admin_form: Page Assignment checklist with all site pages

pages_with_hero.home: 3 slides (Welcome to Legacy, Hero 3, and one more)

custom_page_rendering.url_lookup: Finds pages by URL path from nav_pages
custom_page_rendering.hero_integration: Fetches and renders hero slides assigned to page ID

system_page_hero.behavior: Renders hero slides above page content

404_handling.behavior: Shows 404 page when URL not found in nav_pages

bug_fix_applied.issue: useMemo is not defined error in HomePage.js
bug_fix_applied.fix: Added useMemo to import statement: import React, { useState, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react'
